evidence_summary: Establish the scale and danger of the swift river crossing through a failed raft navigation attempt. The sequence must visually communicate the transition from controlled movement to chaotic separation as the current breaks the group's formation.

The group attempts to navigate a wide, swift river on a makeshift raft. The current proves too strong, sweeping them downstream and resulting in the Scarecrow being separated from the group and stuck on his pole in the middle of the water.
Group is positioned on the raft with determined expressions, navigating the river.
The group is on a drifting raft while the_scarecrow is stuck on his pole in mid-river.
Navigating the current leads to the raft losing the Scarecrow to the water.
